Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

(2 comments)

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

We can merge this into 5.3 ldiskfs patch since this will not be long term supported.

Patch Set 2:

> We can merge this into 5.3 ldiskfs patch since this will not be
 > long term supported.

By that logic, then we should probably wait for 5.4: https://www.kernel.org/category/releases.html

Are any distros picking up 5.2 in the near future?

Patch Set 2:

5.4 is going to be LTS :-( I was really hoping for 5.5 so we could have a linux lustre client in the wild.

Patch Set 2:

I'm fine with holding off ldiskfs changes until 5.4
As it is I needed to refactor a patch for 5.2.x stable

Patch Set 2:

> I'm fine with holding off ldiskfs changes until 5.4
 > As it is I needed to refactor a patch for 5.2.x stable

Does that mean that you will do the same for LU-12634? I want to make it clear though, I do not have any objections to added ldiskfs support to these kernels but it would make more sense if they are applied to the LTS and distro used ones instead.

Patch Set 2:

What is the intended goal for this patch? Are we just going to wait for 5.4 (the LTS kernel)?

Patch Set 2:

I personally since ldiskfs work being pushed forward to encourage more ldiskfs work being pushed upstream to ext4.

Abandoned

in favor of patchset for 5.4

parents.subject: LU-12553  mdc: polling mode for changelog reader

commit.subject: LU-12400 ldiskfs: Initial support for 5.2.0-mainline
commit.message:
LU-12400 ldiskfs: Initial support for 5.2.0-mainline

Add ldiskfs support for 5.2.0 vanilla kernel (ELrepo ML kernel)
Fixes new conflict with __ldiskfs_find_entry
Enable building packages with vanilla kernels on SUSE and RHEL

Test-Parameters: trivial
Cray-bug-id: LUS-7600
Signed-off-by: Shaun Tancheff <stancheff@cray.com>
Change-Id: Ib8927d499ec88d1e85d35dd26cb93794d3168919
